lines: Summary of Applicable Walls and Structure Numbers

Complete hand calculations for three Design Height (DH) cases for generic MSE Abutment Wall.
Complete analysis for full DH range using Excel. Include External Stability, Internal Stability, and
Facing Design. Account for CDOT Structure Backfill Class 1, with live load surcharge.
Global Stability checked by CDOT Geotechical Engineers. Found adequate for DH-RL relationship
Calculations completed by Derek Adams, P.E.

Bridge Foundation (HP Piles) present vertical obstruction behind MSE walls. Discrete strip reinforcement
may be splayed around obstruction. Frame must be designed to accomidate continuous reinforcement
Frame has two components/limit states: Pipe parrallel to MSE wall facing must handle flexure and
shear. Rod perpendicular to MSE wall facing must handle tension.
10" diameter standard, galvanized. ASTM A53, Gr B
HSS Members: Evaluate Square HSS 9 x 9 x 5/16 as alternative to Pipe
Use maximum factored loads (kip / horizontal ft) from internal design. Discrete steel strips do not
requrie frame, so exclude load from analysis. Corresponds to lateral load acting per layer of soil
reifnorcement. Check max load from 3 Z depths, including DH
rod. Equal to max shear in
IO" standard pipe Adequate for Flexure
IO" standard pipe Adequate for Shear
Flexural Capacity Of Square HSS Member
Shear Capacity Of Square HSS Member
10" standard pipe [40.5 lb/ft], and 9x9x5/16 HSS [36.10 lb/ft] are both adequate for frame. HSS is
more efficient (less area / wt per foot), so recommend for construction
Soil and Live Load Surcharge loads acting horizontally on Precast Panel Facing. Soil Reinforcement
Strips act as supports. Design for maximum positive moment between supports, and maximum
negetive moment at supports . Assume discrete reinforcement spaced 2.5ft horizontally and vertically.
As per AASHTO 11.10.6.2.2, the factored tensile load applied to the soil reinforcement connection
at the wall face, To, shall be equal to the maximum factored reinforcement tension. As per
AASHTO 11.10.6.4.1-2, Long Term Facing Stregnth, Tac > To / ( , IS equal to LTDS for soil
#5 @ 6" is Adequate for Horizontal Positive Flexure
#5 @ 6" is Adequate for Vertical Positive Flexure

#5 @ 6" is Adequate for Vertical Negetive Flexure
